name: RDP Service Status Reconnaissance via sc.exe
description: Detects use of sc.exe to query the TermService (Remote Desktop Services) status. Attackers enumerate RDP service state as a prerequisite for lateral movement via RDP (T1021.001).
query: process where process.name == "sc.exe" and process.command_line like~ "*TermService*" and process.command_line like~ "*query*"
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: RDP Registry Configuration Enumeration via reg.exe
description: Detects reg.exe querying Terminal Server registry keys (fDenyTSConnections, UserAuthentication, RDP-Tcp) to enumerate RDP configuration. Attackers use this to verify RDP is accessible before lateral movement (T1021.001).
query: process where process.name == "reg.exe" and process.command_line like~ "*query*" and (process.command_line like~ "*Terminal Server*" or process.command_line like~ "*RDP-Tcp*" or process.command_line like~ "*fDenyTSConnections*" or process.command_line like~ "*UserAuthentication*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: RDP Session Enumeration via qwinsta
description: Detects execution of qwinsta.exe to list active Remote Desktop sessions. Attackers use this to identify active RDP sessions for hijacking or to confirm RDP is in use on a target (T1021.001, T1049).
query: process where process.name in ("qwinsta.exe", "query.exe") and not process.parent.name in ("mmc.exe", "tsadmin.exe", "rdcman.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Windows Credential Manager Manipulation via cmdkey
description: Detects cmdkey.exe adding or listing credentials in Windows Credential Manager. Attackers use cmdkey /add to pre-stage credentials for RDP or SMB connections, facilitating lateral movement without interactive authentication prompts (T1555.004).
query: process where process.name == "cmdkey.exe" and (process.command_line like~ "*/add:*" or process.command_line like~ "*-add:*" or process.command_line like~ "*/list*") and (process.command_line like~ "*/user:*" or process.command_line like~ "*/pass:*" or process.command_line like~ "*/add:*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: RDP Lateral Movement Multi-Stage Recon Chain
description: High-confidence sequence detection: a process spawning RDP recon tools (qwinsta or reg Terminal Server query) followed by cmdkey credential manipulation within 5 minutes on the same host. This sequential pattern is a strong indicator of automated RDP lateral movement preparation (T1021.001, T1555.004).
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=5m
  [process where process.name in ("qwinsta.exe", "sc.exe") and
   (process.name == "qwinsta.exe" or process.command_line like~ "*TermService*")]
  [process where process.name == "cmdkey.exe" and
   process.command_line like~ "*/add:*"]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
